perturbation.original_text: 12.9 WAIVER OF RIGHT TO JURY TRIAL. In connection with the Parties' rights under this Article 12, EACH PARTY, TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY, AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVES ITS RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION OR OTHER LEGAL PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT AND THE TRANSACTIONS IT CONTEMPLATES. THIS WAIVER APPLIES TO ANY ACTION OR LEGAL PROCEEDING, WHETHER SOUNDING IN CONTRACT, TORT, OR OTHERWISE.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.114 WAIVER OF RIGHT TO JURY TRIAL. In connection with the Parties' rights under this Article 12, EACH PARTY, TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY, AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVES ITS RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION OR OTHER LEGAL PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT AND THE TRANSACTIONS IT CONTEMPLATES. THIS WAIVER APPLIES TO ANY ACTION OR LEGAL PROCEEDING, WHETHER SOUNDING IN CONTRACT, TORT, OR OTHERWISE.
perturbation.explanation: The jury trial waiver is moved from the 'Dispute Resolution' section to the 'Definitions' section, where parties may not reasonably look for it. This relocation buries a significant legal clause, making it difficult to find and understand, which impacts its enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Seventh Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While this right can be waived, the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Moving the jury trial waiver to an unexpected section of the contract (Definitions) undermines the knowing and voluntary aspect of the waiver by obscuring it. It may render the waiver unenforceable under certain circumstances if a court finds that it was not sufficiently conspicuous or brought to the attention of the waiving party.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts legal principles related to the knowing and voluntary waiver of the right to a jury trial. The Law Explanation states that moving the jury trial waiver to the 'Definitions' section undermines the 'knowing and voluntary aspect' of the waiver by obscuring it, potentially rendering it unenforceable. This aligns with the Perturbation Explanation, which highlights that relocating the clause buries a significant legal obligation, making it difficult to find and understand, impacting its enforceability. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing the Seventh Amendment, mentions the importance of jury trials in civil cases and suggests that a waiver must be clear. By placing the waiver in an unexpected and less prominent section, the modified text creates a structural flaw that makes the waiver less likely to be considered knowing and voluntary, thus creating a legal contradiction, as defined by 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction'.
